raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command that prints summary of new/missing files and which files differ between two directories, sorted alphabetically. Use diff -qr and then process. Maybe use comm? We can use diff -qr folder1 folder2 | sort. But need summary: new/missing files and which differ. diff -qr gives lines like "Only in folder1: file", "Only in folder2: file", "Files folder1/file and folder2/file differ". So just run diff -qr /system/folder1 /system/folder2 | sort. That prints sorted. So command: diff -qr /system/folder1 /system/folder2 | sort.
raw_output_payload.reasoning_content: We need a bash command that prints summary of new/missing files and which files differ between two directories, sorted alphabetically. Use diff -qr and then process. Maybe use comm? We can use diff -qr folder1 folder2 | sort. But need summary: new/missing files and which differ. diff -qr gives lines like "Only in folder1: file", "Only in folder2: file", "Files folder1/file and folder2/file differ". So just run diff -qr /system/folder1 /system/folder2 | sort. That prints sorted. So command: diff -qr /system/folder1 /system/folder2 | sort.
